label: plain_text
text:
In Fig. 10.1 (i), the line PQ
and the circle have no common
point. In this case,
PQ is called a non-intersecting
 line with respect to the circle. In Fig. 10.1
(ii), there
are two common p
points A and B that the line PQ
and the circle have. In this case, we
call the line PQ
a secant of the circle. In Fig. 10.1
(iii), there is only c
one point A which
is common to the line PQ and the circle. In this case, the line is called a tangent to the
circle.
